Mīr says:-- اس اسيري کي نه کوٴي اي صبا پالي پڙي ايک نظرگل ديکهني کي بهي همين لالي پڙي Is asīrī ke na koʼī, ai ṣabā! pāle paṛe; yak naz̤ar gul dekhne ke bhī hameṅ lāle paṛe. r: lām, l., la, antah-stha, abjad, Shawwāl., laghu., l, r, ṛ
def: the thirtieth letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twenty-seventh of the Persian alphabet, and the twenty-third of the Arabic), has the sound of the English l. The corresponding Hindī letter is ल la, the twenty-eighth consonant of the Devanāgarī alphabet, and the third of the semi-vowels or antah-stha letters. In reckoning by abjad, lām stands for 30; it is also used as an abbreviation for the month Shawwāl. In works on Hindī Prosody, ल la is used as an abbreviation of the word लघु laghu. The letter l is commonly interchanged with r and ṛ, as will have been seen in what has preceded, and as will be pointed out in what follows.
more: the 23rd letter of the Arabic, and 27th of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ल in Sanskrit and Hindī. It is one of the eight dental letters in Arabic, and its sound is that of the English l . In abjad it stands for 30, and, as an abbreviation for the month Shavvāl . As an initial, medial, and final it is interchangeable with t, r, s, k , q. v.; with t , as lag=tak till; sulānā =sutānā put to sleep; rāval=rāvat king; pīlā=pīt , pale: with ṭ , as laiyā=ṭaiyā shell; luppo=ṭuppo cap; kapāl=kapāṭ skull: with d , q. v. as lahaknā=dahaknā to blaze; W. bulbulā = E. budbudā a bubble; kurel=kured scrape; Panj. chugal = P. chugad owl: with n , as E. baṅgā = W. naṅgā naked; lon=non salt'; līl=nīl indigo; phulaṅg=phunaṅg the end of a branch; jalam=janam birth; mallū=manṅū monkey; A. sandal = H. chandan sandal; suhāgal= suhāgan a feme covert . As a medial and final with ṛ , as haldī = Rus. hardī tu…

the twenty-third letter of the Arabic, and twenty-seventh of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ल in Sanskrit. It is one of the eight dental letters in Arabic, and its sound is that of the English l. In the denotation of numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 30: and, by way of abbreviation, it may represent the month شوال. Its permutations are, 1. In Persian and Hindūstānī into ر and vice versa; as, زرو zarū for زلو zalū, A leech; بار for بال Hair; ديوال for ديوار A wall; تلوار for تروار A sword. 2. In Arabic, in the article, into م; as, ليس من امبرمصيام في امسفر laisa mina-m-birri-m-ṣiyāmu fi-m-safari, for ليس من البرالصيام في السفر laisa mina-l-birri-ṣ-ṣiyāmu fi-s-safari, It is not a virtuous act to fast on a journey. In Arabic it may be prefixed to a word, for various purposes: as, 1. Pronounced with the vowel fatʼḥa, and prefixed to the aorist of a verb, for …

def: v.t. more: v. n. S. ला bring. 1. To bring; fetch. 'Tum baṛe sajan kī dhī, jabānī mat karo, jī! Tum par lāūṅgā saut, saukan lāūṅ biyāh-ke, jī! ' Play. ' Ek nahīṅ, do chār, ḍarāī nāëṅ ḍarūṅ, jī mahārāj! ' 'A great man's daughter you forsooth! cease bandying words with me! A co-wife I'll bring home, a wedded co-wife she shall be.' 'Not one, bring three or four, dont think, my Lord, you'll frighten me!' 2. To bring forward; carry over. 3. To introduce; usher in. 4. To lead; draw or bring over; win over. 5. ( andar lānā ) To import. 6. ( dar lānā ) To bring under; include; comprehend. 7. To produce; yield; breed; beget. 1. Raṅg lānā . 2. Fail lānā . 8. To pimp. 9. To purchase; buy. Jis bhāo lānā usī bhāo bechnā . We sell as we buy. gair mulk se gulām ke taur par lānā . G. G. To import as a slave.
v. n. 1. To bring, adduce, induce, introduce, persuade, apply. 2. To breed, produce, make.

wety: Two theories, or conflation of both: * A reduction from the light verb construction لے آنا (le ānā) with the same meaning, explaining the lack of an ergative marker for its subject as آنا (ānā) is intransitive. Compare Punjabi ਲਿਆਉਣਾ (liāuṇā) which more transparently is a lexicalized light verb construction, preserving the original vowels. Nepali ल्याउनु (lyāunu), Gujarati લાવવું (lāvvũ) may also reflect the same.…

def: s.f. A quantity, measure; a heap, large quantity, much; — loins, reins; — bird-lime; — chaff; wheat (or any small cereal) cut in the straw (in this sense prob. fr. S. लाव+कृ).
s.m. A heap, a large quantity, much; — s.f. Loin, reins
more: n. m. A heap; store; large quantity. Paisā hī paisā kar-ke laṅk lagtā hai. laṅk lagnā , v. n. To be piled; heaped.
s. f. The reins, the loins.
subs: (-kā), A heap, or pile (of things) to be formed; — to be heaped, or piled.

def: s.f. Name of the chief town in Ceylon (renowned as the capital of Rāvaṇa); — name of the island of Ceylon; — name of the place whence Hindū astronomers calculate longitude (which is, according to some accounts, distinct from Ceylon)
more: s. Name of the capital of Rāvaṇa in Ceylon.
subs: and laṅkā-pati, s.m. = lankeśvar, q.v. below
s.m. Name of the sixth section of the Rāmāyaṇ (which describes the invasion and conquest of Laṅkā by Rām-ćandra)
vulg. laṅkes (˚kā+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of laṅkā,' an epithet of any king of Laṅkā, but especially of Rāvaṇ.

def: s.f. The gramineous plant Trigonella corniculata.
s.f. The gramineous plant Trigonella corniculata.
s.f. Leap, jump, spring, bound (cf. lapak and lapkā).

ety: S. लण्डं; cf. also S. दण्डः
def: s.m. Membrum virile, penis
subs: Anything used up and unfit for service.

def: s.m. = lanḍ, q.v. s.m. = lāṅṛ = laṇḍ, qq.v. s.m. Membrum virile, penis
subs: Anything used up and unfit for service.

def: n. m. A potherb ( Chironia cenlanroides ). (?)
(lit. thou shalt never see me) s. f. Boasting, airs, conceitedness.

s.f. Boasting, gasconading; airs, conceitedness. (The words are taken from the Qǒrʼān, Sūra vii. 149, al aʻrāf, 'And when Moses came at our appointed time, and his Lord spake unto him, he said, "O Lord, show thyself to me, that I may behold thee" (rabbi arinī, anz̤ur ilai-ka): He said, "Thou shalt not see Me (lan tarā-nī); but look towards the mount, and if it stand firm in its place, then shalt thou see Me" ')
more: n. f. lit . you cant see me. Boasting ( shekhī ).

def: s.m. Blockhead, fool.
s.m. Cutting, cutting down; reaping; — the painted quail, Perdix chinensis (see lawā).
n. m. P. lavā flag. An army with its baggage and Camp followers.
s. m. (dakh.) Army.

s.m. A rope; the rope by which the ćaras or leathern bucket is drawn up from a well (syn. bart); cable (of a boat); — the quantity of land irrigated by one lāʼo in a day (in some places the quantity of land irrigated is reckoned by the lāʼo, instead of by wells, and is estimated at fifteen acres to a lāʼo); — a debt secured by a pledge
more: n. m. 1. The rope by which the leather bucket of a well is drawn up. In some places the amount of land irrigated is reckoned by the lāo , instead of wells, being commonly 15 acres to a lāo . 2. The quantity of land irrigated by one lāo in one day. 3. The cable of a boat. 4. Demand; request. 5. A debt secured by a pledge. lāo uṭhānā , v. n. To advance money to a cultivator for food, fodder, and seed. lāo charas , n. Irrigation by water raised from wells in the bucket called Charas . It is called purhāī when cattle are used, and garrā when manual labor is employed. Carnegy. lāo chalānā , v. n. To irrigate from a well. lāo lagānā , v. n. To assert a right to, and take over from a debtor property of any kind in satisfaction of a debt. lāo lagānā , n. m. Payment in kind.
s. m. 1. A rope. 2. The cable of a boat.
subs: v.n. To advance money to a cultivator for food fodder and seed
s.m. The rope and leathern bucket (for irrigation); irrigation by water raised from wells in the ćaras or leathern bucket
'To work the well-rope,' to irrigate from a well
(-par), To assert a right (to), and take over (from a debtor) property (of any kind) in satisfaction of a debt; — s.m. Payment in kind.

def: s.m. = lāba, or lāwa, qq.v. s.m. Request, desire; supplication, prayer; — ridicule, irony; a jest, joke, anything facetious; — adulation, flattery, coaxing.
s.m. Banner, standard
subs: s.m. An army with its baggage and camp-followers.

ety: said to be by redupl. of lāʼo, imperat. pl. of lānā, 'to bring,' but prob. S. lābha, redupl.
def: s.m. Greediness, covetousness, avarice.

ety: lāʼo = lāb = lāh = S. लाभः+sāʼo, a rhyming word
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage.

ety: S. लिप्ता or लिप्तं, rt. ली; cf. lapaṭnā, lapeṭnā
def: s.f. The space formed in the palm of the hand when closed (so as to hold anything); a handful; as much as can be held in both palms joined together
more: n. f. A handful; as much as can be held in both palms joined together. lap, yā lābh kāṛh-ke . A handful less. Lābh kāṛh-ke dūṅgā, chāhe le, chāhe nā le .
s. f. A handful, the space in the palm of the hand closed so as to hold water.

def: n. m. Lapping (as a dog). lap lap , adv. Quickly. lap lap k. , v. n. To lap (as a dog). lap lap khānā , v. n. To eat quickly.
more: s. m. Lapping (as, of a dog drinking). ¶ lap-lap, adj. Nimble, quick. لپ لپ کهانا lap-lap khānā, To eat quickly.

def: s.f. Spring, bound; bounce; a flash (of lightning, &c.), dart; a snatch; — beat, throb, pulsation, palpitation; — springiness, elasticity, flexibility; nimbleness, dexterity, swiftness; — a bad habit
more: n. f. 1. A flash (of lightning, etc.). 2. A spring; bound. 3. Beat; pulsation; throb; palpitation. 4. ( lapak jhapak ) Swiftness; nimbleness; dexterity. 5. Elasticity, flexibility. 6. A bad habit. lapak jhapak se , adv. Swiftly; dexterously.
(from لپکنا) s. f. 1. A flash (of lightning, &c.). 2. The spring (of a tiger, &c.), bounce, snatch.
subs: adv. With a spring or bound; with a hop, skip, and a jump; nimbly, briskly, swiftly, dexterously.

ety: lapak˚ = Prk. लप्पक्क(इ) or लप्पक्के(इ), prob. fr. S. लम्फ+कृ
def: v.n. To spring (upon, — par), to dart (at), to snatch (at); to make an attack (on); to bound, bounce; to catch (a ball, &c.); to flash, dart (as lightning); to beat, throb, palpitate; to rush (after), to give chase (to); to hurry, make haste
more: v. a. 1. E. To flash (as lightning). Bijlī lapaktī hai . 2. To beat; pulsate; palpitate; throb. Is vaqt nabz khūb lapak rahī hai . 3. To spring; bound ( uchhalnā , 1). 4. To make haste; run; give chase ( dauṛnā , 1); to put one's best leg foremost. 5. To spring upon; attack. 6. To catch (a ball). 7. To seize; grasp; snatch; gripe; clutch. Upar kī ūpar hī lapak lī . 8. To snap up; snap at; hook; claw.
v. n. To flash (as, lightning), to spring, bounce or rush forth or upon, to attack.
subs: v.t. To spring forward and seize; to seize, snatch; to snap at or up; to hook, claw.

def: adj. Slender, thin, slight, delicate.
n. f. 1. Jargon; jumble. 2. Perversion of the truth; prevarication; equivocation; suggestio falsī; suppressio veri. lapaṛ shapaṛ , adv. Awkwardly.

ety: lapaṛ = lapaṭ, q.v.+śapar, a rhyming word
def: s.f. Involved or confused speech; jargon, jumble; perversion of the truth; prevarication; equivocation; — adv. In a confused or jumbled manner, incoherently; — awkwardly.

ety: prob. fr. lipaṭ; S. लिप्ता or लिप्तं; cf. lapeṭ
def: s.f. A withering blast (as of hot wind, &c.); blast; shock; a coup-de-soleil; — flame, blaze; heat, warmth, glow (of fire, or flame, &c.); — sweet smell, scent, odour (syn. mahak)
subs: s.f. Fawning; wriggling, writhing, &c. (cf. lapar-śapaṛ, and lapeṭ-sapeṭ).

def: s.f. Tattling; lying; — s.m. = labāṛī = labāṛ, q.v. s.f. Falsehood, untruth, duplicity, dishonesty, knavery; a lie, fabrication, forgery, fraud; plundering, plunder; — (dialec.) meanness; silliness.
n. m. S. लप See labār .

s.m. = lapāṭī = labāṛī = labāṛ, q.v. s.f. Tattling; lying; — s.m. = labāṛī = labāṛ, q.v. s.f. Falsehood, untruth, duplicity, dishonesty, knavery; a lie, fabrication, forgery, fraud; plundering, plunder; — (dialec.) meanness; silliness.
more: s. m. A tattler, a liar.

def: s.m. = lapāṭiyā = labāṛiyā = labāṛ, q.v. s.m. = lapāṭī = labāṛī = labāṛ, q.v. s.f. Tattling; lying; — s.m. = labāṛī = labāṛ, q.v. s.f. Falsehood, untruth, duplicity, dishonesty, knavery; a lie, fabrication, forgery, fraud; plundering, plunder; — (dialec.) meanness; silliness.
s.f. The skate (fish).

n. f. 1. Cover; covering; envelope. 2. A coil; twist; bandage; fillet (طي). 3. Circumference. 4. Ambiguity. 5. Entanglement; complication. 6. Difficulty; hardship. 7. Perturbation; consternation. 8. Deception ( buttā ). 9. Loss; damage. lapeṭ-jhapeṭ, lapeṭ-sapeṭ , n. f. 1. Jumble; confusion; disorder. 2. Evasion; subterfuge.

r: lapeṭ, lapeṭnā, āṅdhī-kā lapeṭ, coup-de-soleil, cold, lapeṭ-jhapeṭ, lapeṭ-sapeṭ
def: s.f. Involved state, confusion, jumble, disorder; evasion, subterfuge
more: (from لپيٿنا) s. f. m. A fold, a ply, envelope, twist, bandage, fillet. لپيٿ جهپيٿ يا سپيٿ lapeṭ-jhapeṭ or -sapeṭ, s. f. Evasion, subterfuge.

ety: lapeṭ, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.), Cover, covering, &c. ( = lapeṭ); — a glass bracelet having gold or silver thread wound round it ( = lapeṭwāṅ).
(f. -ī), perf. part. of lapeṭnā, q.v.
n. 1. A roller on which cloth is wound in the loom. 2. The beam of a cotton mill.
s.f. A roller on which cloth is wound in the loom; a button; — the beam (of a cotton-mill).
more: s. f. A roller on which cloth is wound in the loom, a button, &c.

def: n. m. Cor. of Rough rider.
v. a. See lipaṭnā , v. n.

r: lapeṭnā, lapaṭna, lipaṭnā, eṭ, ćapeṭa, ćapaṭa
ety: caus. of lapaṭna or lipaṭnā, q.v.; for eṭ, cf. S. ćapeṭa and ćapaṭa
def: v.t. To roll up; to roll or wrap (round, -par), to wind or twine (round), to furl; to wrap up, to enwrap, to envelop; to cover by wrapping round; to fold, coil, enclose, pack; to involve, implicate; — to smear, to spread (over, -par)
more: v. a. To wrap up, to fold, to enclose, to pack, to roll, to furl, to spread.
subs: and lapeṭ-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = lapeṭnā.

ety: lapeṭ + wāṅ = Prk. अअं (with w inserted) = S. क+कं
def: adj. & s.m. Wrapped up; enveloped; folded; involved; twined, twisted, coiled; ornamented with gold or silver thread wound round; indirect (speech), roundabout, ambiguous; hidden, secret; — involved or ambiguous speech (syn. gol bāt), innuendo; — a glass bracelet having gold or silver thread wound round it; — adv. Indirectly, ambiguously, by way of innuendo.
more: adj. Twisted, ornamented with gold or silver thread wound round it.

def: adj. 1. Wrapped up; folded. 2. Twined; twisted; coiled. 3. Ornamented with gold or silver thread wound round it. 4. Hidden; secret; latent. Lapeṭvāṅ gālī . 5. Roundabout; indirect. Lapeṭvāṅ bāt. lapeṭvāṅ , adv. Indirectly; by way of inuendo.
v.n. To jump, leap, spring.

adj. & adv. 'Going by springs and bounds'; rapid and bouncing (as a gait); active, nimble, dapper, quick; — nimbly, briskly, quickly, smartly; — s.f. Nimbleness, briskness, dexterity; — pouncing upon and carrying off, pilfering, stealing.

adj. Nimble; quick; fast. Bāp chup chup, pūt lap-jhap . Prov. The father so quiet, the son so sharp. lap jhap , adv. Quickly; smartly; sharp; in a trice; nimbly. Rāṅdhūṅ, rī dhī merī, motī chhaṛā bhāt, harī harī mūṅg disāorī, Phulke to, merī dhī, lap jhap poūṅ, ghī bhar ḍālūṅ ṭoknā! Song. Marvan. I'll cook rice pearly white, Daughter! with green dāl bright and rare, The cakes I'll briskly bake, Daughter! nor keg of butter spare. lap-jhap , n. f. 1. Dexterity; nimbleness; swiftness. Yeh aurat apnī lap jhap se har ek chīz churā letī hai . 2. Stealing; pilfering; cribbing. Muglāniyāṅ kabhī apnī lap jhap se bāz nahīṅ ātīṅ. lap-jhap chāl , n. f. A rapid awkward gait. Aisī lap jhap chāl chaltā hai ki ek na ek chīz gir paṛtī hai . Wom. lupar lupar h., lup lup k., luplupānā , Slang, v. n. To beat or throb (the heart with fear).
s.m. A bound forward (in order to snatch); a snatch; — nimbleness, &c. ( = lapak); — a bad habit; a vitiated taste; — one who snatches anything from another
more: n. m. 1. A bad habit. Laṛātā hai muā jis tis se āṅkheṅ, Use be-tarah yeh lapkā paṛā hai . Wom. Rangin. 2. A vitiated taste. 3. ( Brij .) One who snatches anything from another. 4. A loose character; a vagabond. lapkā paṛnā , v. n. To be addicted to a bad habit. Use to harām ke māl kā lapkā paṛ gayā hai .

s. m. 1. A snatch. 2. Nimbleness. 3. A bad custom or habit. Mīr says: اپني توجـﮩان آنکهـ لڙي پهر وهين ديکهو آينه کو لپکا هي پريشان نظري کا Upnī to jahāṅ ānkh laṛī phir wahīṅ dekho; āʼīne ko lapkā hai pareshān-naz̤arī kā. Where I first fell in love, there you will see me; (I am not like) the mirror (which) is in the habit of casting its glances in all directions.
subs: (-kā), To be addicted (to, a bad habit).

def: v.t. To bound forward in order to snatch; to snatch at, dart at, make a snatch for; to stretch forth (the hand, for a thing); — to despatch (one) quickly (in this sense, also, lapkā-denā).
more: v. 1. To stretch forth one's hand for something. 2. To despatch one quickly. Ek ādmī lapkā do!

def: s.f. A long stitch; a stitch; basting, sewing; — lapkī bharnā or lapkī mārnā (-meṅ), To stitch, to baste.
more: n. f. A stitch; sewing. lapkī bharnā, yā mārnā , v. a. To stitch; baste.

ety: prob. fr. S. लम्फ by redupl.
def: adj. & adv. Nimble, quick, smart; — nimbly, quickly (syn. lap-jhap)
s.m. Lapping (as, of a dog drinking); — beating, throbbing, palpitation (of the heart, from fear, &c.)

ety: a dialec. form. of labāṛiyā, q.v.
def: s.m. A flatterer; cajoler.

ety: prob. S. लेप+कः, or लेप्य+कं, rt. लिप्
def: s.m. Ornamental work (on cloth) with gold or silver thread; gold or silver cloth, brocade
more: n. m. 1. Brocade; gold or silver thread. 2. The inverted position of a stick at geṛī , q. v. Lappā na ṭūṭe! Dont turn over the stick!
s. m. Brocade, gold or silver cloth.
subs: adj. Having a border of gold or silver thread; brocaded; embroidered.

def: n. f. Scratching and biting ( nochā khasoṭī ); a tussle; boxing; fighting. lappā-ḍuk'kī k . To fight; have a set to.
s.m. A slap, thump, buffet, cuff (syn thappaṛ).
n. m. See thappaṛ , 1.
s.f. A glutinous kind of food, pottage, pap, spoon-diet.
more: n. f. S. लप्सिका 1. Pap; farinaceous food. 2. Spoon diet. lapsī kā pinḍ , n. m. A ricketty old man.
s. f. A glutinous kind of food, pottage, pap, spoonmeat.

def: s.m. Connection, relation; — a kind of grass, Panicum verticillatum; — a kind of thin molasses; — any glutinous liquid food (syn. lapṭī, lapṛī, lapsī).
more: s. m. 1. A kind of molasses. 2. Relation, connexion. 3. Name of a grass. (Panicum verticillatum). 4. See لپٿي lapṭī.

def: s.f. Pap; any glutinous liquid food (syn. lupṛī, lapsī).
more: s. f. Pap, any glutinous liquid food.

def: s.m.(f.?) Saliva, spittle, slaver, dribbling, dribble of an infant (syn. rāl).
more: n. f. 1. A line; row. Ūṅṭoṅ kī lār . 2. E. Saliva ( rāl ).
wety: From Sanskrit लाला (lālā).

def: s.f. (dialec.) A continuous line, a string, series, row, train; — adv. In the train (of), in the rear (of), behind; in company (with).
wety: From Sanskrit लाला (lālā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of lāṛ, q.v. s.m.(f.?) Saliva, spittle, slaver, dribbling, dribble of an infant (syn. rāl).
wety: From Sanskrit लाला (lālā).

ety: S. लाड, rt. लाड् = लड्; or S. लाल, fr. caus. of rt. लल्
def: s.m. Affection, lovingness, tenderness; coaxing, caressing, caress, endearment; blandishment, wooing, courtship; sport, play; playfulness (of a child)
more: s. m. Lovingness, coaxing, caress, play, sport, playfulness of a child. لاڙلڙانا lāṛ laṛānā, To fondle (a child).
subs: s.m. Fondness and affection; great fondness; love and dalliance
s.m. Lār and Kapūr, two celebrated merry minstrels of the court of Akbar
or lāṛ laṛānā (-se), To fondle, to caress.

def: s.m. A measuring-rod (for measuring land, — varying from 4½ to 6 or 6½ cubits.
more: n. m. A measuring rod for land measure, varying from 4½ to 6 or 6½ cubits.

n. f. S. यष्टि 1. A string (of pearls, etc). 2. A strand of a rope or cord. 3. A row; line; series. 4. A chain. 5. A party ( thok , 1) 6. ( Bot .) Amentum; catkin. laṛ milānā , v. a. To form a friendship with. laṛ meṅ rahnā , v. n. To be on one's side; to remain with; to be in one's pay.

def: s.f. A string (of pearls, &c.); a thread; a strand (of a rope or cord); a row, line, series; a chain; — a party, side; (in Bot.) ament, catkin
more: s. f. 1. A string (of pearls, &c.), a thread, a row. 2. A strand of a rope or cord. 3. A party. لڙ مين رهنا laṛ meṅ rahnā, To be in oneʼs party.
subs: s.m. pl. Relations, kinsmen, family, children; — a parasite
(-kī), To be in the party (of), be on the side (of); to remain (with); to be in the pay (of).

def: adj. Of (one) string; of (two, or three, &c.) strings (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ek-laṛā, 'of one string'; pać-laṛā, 'of five strings'; sat-laṛā, 'of seven strings': — larā is also used to form reduplicatives).

s.m. corr. of lāḍā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Beloved, pet, darling; tenderly nurtured; — a beloved object, a pet; a favourite; a lover, sweetheart; a bridegroom; — a character in a play; — raw indigo-plant (as it comes into the factory)
subs: Lover and beloved, lovers; bridegroom and bride.

def: s.f. Fighting, wrestling; fight, contention, quarrel, squabble, brawl; conflict, combat, battle, war; discord, hostility, enmity
subs: To extend a quarrel; to foment or aggravate a quarrel
Strife or war to be imminent
(-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to set (people) by the ears; to make (dogs, &c.) fight

adj. (f. -kī), Of or relating to war, warlike, martial (e.g. laṛāʼī-kā bājā 'martial music'; laṛāʼī-kā jahāz, 'war-ship,' 'man-of-war'; laṛāʼī-kā sāmān, 'materials of war, ammunition'; — laṛāʼī-kā ghar, 'the abode of strife; cause of quarrel; a firebrand; an incendiary'; laṛāʼī-kā gīt, 'war-song')
(-se), To wrangle or quarrel (with); to fight, make war; to give battle (to)
(-se), To fight (with), to wage or carry on war
(-se), To engage in combat or a fight
v.n. A quarrel to exist (between, -meṅ), to be at strife or war
or desī laṛāʼī, A civil war.
wety: Related to لڑنا (laṛnā, “to fight; argue”).

def: s. f. Battle, quarrel, war, fight, fighting. لڙاٴي کرنا laṛāʼī karnā, To war, to fight, to give battle.

n. f. See rāṛ . 1. Contention; quarrel; brawl. 1. Jorū khasam kī laṛāī, dūdh kī sī malāī . Prov. Wives and husbands quarrel away, Cream will rise skim as you may. 2. Khālī ādmī kahā kare, ghar meṅ baiṭh laṛāī laṛe? Prov. A lone man in the house, what can he do but quarrel and fight? 2. Conflict; contest; skirmish; affray; warfare. Laṛāī bhiṛāī par khāk ḍālo, apne dam kī khair māṅgo! Your quarrels bury, a blessing crave! 3. Wrestling; boxing; pugilism; fight; fighting; combat. 4. Battle; war; campaign. 5. Hostility; enmity. Sārī paise kī laṛāī hai . Money is the root of quarrels. laṛāī bāṅdhnā , v. a. To make a quarrel. laṛāī baṛhānā , v. a. See rāṛ baṛhānā. laṛāī palle bāṅdhnā, yā mol lenā , v. a. lit . to buy a quarrel. To involve oneself in a fray or quarrel. 1. Udhār denā, laṛāī mol lenā . Prov. Give a loan, a quarrel own. 2. Apnā do, aur laṛāī mol lo! Prov. Give of your own, a quarrel buy! (Lend your money and lose your friend). (A. Al-qarz miqrāz-ul-mohabbat ). 3. Sach kahnā ādhī laṛāī mol lenā hai . Prov. To speak the truth is to purchase half a quarrel. laṛāī pe jānā, yā chaṛhnā , v. a. To go to war. laṛāī tulnā . To be imminent (a war). Jab tul gaī laṛāī tarāzā kī tol par Bāṭoṅ se bāṭ phūṭe, dhaṛoṅ se dhaṛe laṛe . Inshā. laṛāī ṭhānnā , v. n. To be decided on (a war). laṛāī kā bājā , Martial music. laṛāī kā jahāz , n. A war ship; man-of-war. laṛāī kā sāmān . The materials of war; ammunition. laṛāī kā ghar , n. m. 1. A firebrand; an incendiary. 2. The cause of quarrel. laṛāī kā gīt , War song. laṛāī kā maidān . A battlefield. laṛāī k. , Wom. laṛāī mārnā , v. a. 1. To wrangle; quarrel. 2. To fight; contend. laṛāī laṛnā , v. a. To wage or carry on war. laṛāī h. , v. n. 1. To have a quarrel. 2. To be at war. mulkī laṛāī , n. f. A foreign war. desī yā andarūnī laṛāī . A civil war.

ety: laṛ˚, rt. of laṛnā+ait, as in lanḍait, q.v.
def: s.m. = laṛantiyā, laṛwaiyā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, pugnacious; — athletic; — a great fighter; a wrestler, a champion.

ety: laṛkā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं त्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं
def: s.m. The condition of being a child, the state of childhood; boyhood, childhood.

n. m. See bāl-pan. Chal, tohe piyā ne bulāī, bahut rahī bābal ghar, dulhan! Khel anokhe khele sakhiyan saṅg, bahut karī larkāī . A dirge. Go, bride! thy husband summons thee—too long Under thy father's roof hast thou delayed, Rare sport hast thou had with thy maiden throng, And all too much the game of childhood played. laṛak-budh , n. f. Childishness ( bāl-budh ). He lalā! abahīṅ tihārī laṛak-budhi nahīṅ gaī, siyāne hau to kā? Farrukh. Sir! you have not yet thrown off your childishness. What does it signify that you are grown in years?
s. m. Childhood, childishness.

ety: Prk. लडावको, and लडावकओ; S. रट+आपि (caus. augment) + अकः and अक+ कः

Farrukh. n. f. Pasture ground.

more: v. a. 1. (causal of لڙنا r. लड Agitate) To make fight, to fight, to bait. 2. (r. लड Sport, dally) To play.
v. a. (dakh.) Perhaps for لٿانا, q. v.
v. a. (dakh.) causal of لڙنا liṛnā, which see below.

def: v. a. Caus. of لرَنا q. v.

def: s.f. Fighting; wrestling; contending, &c. (see laṛnā); — (Prk. लडंतं) adj. Contentious, quarrelsome, pugnacious ( = laṛāk)
n. f. Fighting; wrestling. laṛant k. , v. a. To wrestle; fight. laṛant'iyā, laṛait , n. m. A wrestler; champion. laṛantiyā , adj. Athletic. laṛne ko jī chāhnā , v. n. To wish to fight.
adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, pugnacious; — athletic; — a great fighter; a wrestler, a champion.

Strife or war to be imminent

adj. (f. -kī), Of or relating to war, warlike, martial (e.g. laṛāʼī-kā bājā 'martial music'; laṛāʼī-kā jahāz, 'war-ship,' 'man-of-war'; laṛāʼī-kā sāmān, 'materials of war, ammunition'; — laṛāʼī-kā ghar, 'the abode of strife; cause of quarrel; a firebrand; an incendiary'; laṛāʼī-kā gīt, 'war-song')
(-se), To wrangle or quarrel (with); to fight, make war; to give battle (to)
(-se), To fight (with), to wage or carry on war
(-se), To engage in combat or a fight
v.n. A quarrel to exist (between, -meṅ), to be at strife or war
or desī laṛāʼī, A civil war.

adj. (f. -ī), Mad, foolish.

ety: lāṛhiyā, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)

s.f. A string (of pearls, or flowers, &c.); a link (see laṛ).
n. f. A link.

adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = lāṛlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Darling, dear, beloved; tenderly nurtured; petted, spoilt (child); — a darling, a pet; one tenderly nurtured (syn. nāz-parwarda; nāznīn); a spoilt child.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), Boy, son; child, infant, babe
more: s. m. A boy, child, infant, babe. لڙکا بالا laṛkā-bālā, s. m. Child. لڙکا لڙکي laṛkā laṛkī, s. Boys and girls, children.
subs: or laṛkā-wālā, s.m. A child; children
To give birth to a son
s.m. pl. Children, young folk, family, household
Boys and girls, children, bairns, brats
s.m. (f. -wālī), The father of a boy, or of the bridegroom
ex: یہ لڑکا اتنا لمبا نہیں ہے جتنی اسکی بہن ہے۔
yêh laṛkā itnā lambā nahī̃ hai jitnī uskī bahan hai.
This boy is not as tall as his sister.
یہ لڑکا مجھے ایک آنکھ نہیں بھاتا۔
yêh laṛkā mujhe ek ā̃kh nahī̃ bhātā.
I don't like this boy at all.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *लडिक्क (laḍikka). Related to the lexicographically-attested Sanskrit लाडिक (lāḍika, “boy, servant”), लट्व (laṭva, “dancing boy”) लट (laṭa, “fool”), and thus may ultimately come from Sanskrit लट् (laṭ, “to act like a child”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Cognate to Awadhi लरिका (larikā), Bhojpuri लईका (laīkā), Bengali লেড়কা (leṛoka).

def: n. m. S. लड् to play, or S. लट् to be a child. See bachchā (2, 3). A boy; son. 1. Laṛkoṅ meṅ laṛkā, būṛhoṅ meṅ būṛhā . Prov. Saying. With children a child, with old men an old man. 2. Binā chūchī bārah baras laṛkā rakkhe . Prov. The child for twelve years kept without the breast. (To delude with false hopes). 3. Bāīṅ bagal mero laṛkā sove, ochhī khāṭ bipat moko, Sarkat āve, ṭhaur kahāṅ to ko ? Rus. Wom. song. 4. Ham to janaliau hahīṅ ge laṛikvā? torā pāle baṛ baṛ bheḍ, Kiryā khailā se ham nahīṅ manbau, debau nai-harvā raged! Maggah Song. I took you for a child, great art beneath your skirt you hide, Your oaths I'll not believe, back to your father's house you'll stride. 5. Laṛkoṅ kā khel, chiṛyoṅ kā maran . Prov. What's sport for the boys is death to the birds. 6. Laṛke ko jab bheṛyā le gayā, tab ṭaṭṭī bāṅdhī . Prov. After the wolf has carried off the child a matted door is put on. (When the horse was stolen the stable door was shut). 7. Bagal meṅ laṛkā, shahr meṅ ḍhaṅḍhorā . Prov. The child in arms, the crier cries 'Lost child.' laṛkā , adj. See bachchah. laṛkā bālā , n. m. Children ( bachchah ). Kuchh laṛkā-bālā hone-vālā hai . She expects (a child.) laṛkā jannā , v. n. To give birth to a son; to be delivered of a son. Laṛkā jane bībī, aur paṭṭī bāṅdhe miyāṅ! Prov. The wife delivered of a child, the bandage round the husband's head! (One bears the pains, another has the gains). laṛkā god lenā , v. a. To adopt (a son). laṛkoṅ kā khel . See bachchoṅ kā khel. laṛke-bāloṅ kī khabar na lenā . To neglect wife and children. laṛke-bāle . Family; household. laṛke-vālā , n. m. laṛke-vālī , n. f. The father of the bridegroom. rāh bāṭ kā laṛkā . A foundling.
s.f. = laṛakpan, q.v. s.m. The condition of being a child, the state of childhood; boyhood, childhood.
s. f. Childhood, boyhood, the being of a child or children.
E. n. f. Childhood.
s.m. Name of a mountain tribe in Chatīsgarh.
s.m. = laṛakpan, q.v. s.m. The condition of being a child, the state of childhood; boyhood, childhood.

Tir. n. f. The mother of a child. 1. Masurī ke dār jarā halke pakaihā! Tūṅ laṛkorī, jarā baṛh-ke khaihā! Hamrī kadar jān jarā bāsī bhī rakhihā! Bhoj. Prov. (Thrift and liberality). Just cook a little thin the dāl Massur , As a mother you should eat a little more, Put by enough for me some stale dāl o'er! 2. Laṛkā rove, khasam chillāë, laṛkaurī mahirvā phajīhat hoë . Prov. The child cries, the husband roars, the mother gets abuse.
v.n. (dialec.) = laṛkhaṛānā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To stagger, to trip. 2. To stutter, to stammer.

more: v. n. 1. To stagger, to trip. 2. To stutter, to stammer.

def: v. n. 1. To stammer ( laṛbaṛānā ). 2. To reel; stagger ( ḍigmigānā , 1).

ety: laṛ˚ = Prk. लड(इ) = S. रट(ति), rt. रट्
def: v.n. To fight, quarrel, strive, contend, struggle; to make war, give battle; to cope or vie (with); to hit, strike (against), collide (with); to tally, agree, coincide (with, — as an account, &c.); to contend (with); to risk, stake (e.g. jān laṛ rahī hai); to subscribe, contribute (in opposition or rivalry); — fighting, quarrelling, &c.
more: v. n. To fight, quarrel, contend, battle, war. subst. m. Fighting.
v. (dakh.) To daggle, to draggle, to grovel. لڙجانا liṛ jānā, To succumb ( Harris ).
v. n. (dakh.) To roll: see لڙهنا luṛhnā.
subs: v.n. To engage and fight; to fight, quarrel, &c. ( = laṛnā)
v.n. To fall to fighting; to fall out
v.n. To die fighting; to engage in mortal strife
s.m. (f. -wālī), A great fighter; one who engages in mortal combat; one who will fight to the death.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit रटति (raṭati). Compare Gujarati લડવું (laḍvũ), Marathi लढणे (laḍhṇe).

def: Rus. v. a. See ḍālnā .

v. a. See jhagaṛnā , (1, 2). 1. To struggle; contend; contest. 2. To clash with; jar against; cope or vie with. 3. To wrangle; quarrel; fight. 1. Aur woh jo āshnā se jhagṛī haiṅ, yā laṛī haiṅ, Muṅh ko chhupā palaṅg pe machlī huī paṛī haiṅ . Nazīr. And those who with their lovers have a quarrel had In sullen mood their faces hide and lie in bed. 2. Do laṛte haiṅ to ek girtā hī hai . Prov. When two fight one must fall. 4. To go to war; wage war; give battle. Laṛe na bhiṛe, zirah pahne phire! Prov. No stomach for a fight, in armour struts a knight! 5. To hit; strike against; collide with ( bhiṛnā , 2). 6. E. To bite; sting. Tataiyā laṛ gayā . 7. To agree or coincide with. Hisāb laṛnā . 8. To be staked, risked. Jān laṛ rahī hai . 9. To subscribe; contribute one's quota. Āo bhaī! kaë kaë paise laṛte haiṅ? laṛ paṛnā , v. n. To fall out.
